summary: The patient likely has eclampsia due to her recent history including a cesarean section under conditions suggestive of hypertension, presenting two days later with headaches followed by seizures without significant proteinuria; these features are consistent with late-onset eclamptic manifestations as indicated by both neurological signs and radiological abnormalities fitting an acute brain injury pattern characteristic of this condition. Given all factors, especially timing relative to childbirth despite being somewhat delayed from peak risk periods typically associated more closely around labor/delivery itself, eclampsia remains strongly suspected over alternative diagnoses like primary cerebral venous sinus thrombosis or subarachnoid haemorrhage among others considered less probable within context provided herein. To confirm diagnosis further testing such as serum electrolytes levels could also help rule out metabolic causes contributing similarly towards similar symptomatology observed clinically thus far
